hotels.name: Onsen Ryokan Yuen Shinjuku
hotels.notes: Modern ryokan with 18F open-air rooftop onsen overlooking Tokyo skyline. Quiet east-Shinjuku block, 5 min from Shinjuku-Gyoemmae Metro. Yukatas + traditional Japanese breakfast included.

hotels.name: Kozantei Ubuya
hotels.notes: Lakefront ryokan on Ubuyazaki cape (north shore promontory). Two nights (Nov 27-29) before splurging on FUFU final Fuji night.

hotels.name: FUFU Kawaguchiko
hotels.notes: Splurge final Fuji night. Small luxury ryokan, all-suite, every room has a private outdoor onsen. Mt. Fuji views, Bang & Olufsen, Dyson, kaiseki dining.

hotels.name: Hotel TOU Nishinotoin Kyoto
hotels.notes: 4-star wabi-sabi boutique, 10-min walk from Kyoto Station, 8-min from Gojo Station (Karasuma Line). Tighter budget than The Thousand Kyoto to offset the FUFU splurge.

activities.title: Narita Airport (NRT)
activities.description: Land at NRT Terminal 1 (or 2). Customs + baggage claim typically 30-60 min for international arrivals. After clearing, drop checked bags at the Yamato Transport (Kuroneko Takkyubin) counter in Arrivals — next-day delivery to Yuen Shinjuku at ~¥2,500-3,500/bag. Then proceed to the rail station beneath the terminal.
activities.address: Narita International Airport Terminal 1, Narita, Chiba 282-0004

activities.title: NRT Airport Station — board N'EX
activities.description: After customs (~30-60 min) + Yamato luggage drop at the Arrivals counter, head down to the rail level beneath Terminal 1 (~5 min walk from arrivals). N'EX departs ~half-hourly to Shinjuku — ¥3,250pp, ~85 min direct, no transfers.
activities.address: Narita Airport Station (under Terminal 1), Narita, Chiba

routeToNext.pinned: N'EX scheduled time, direct Narita Airport Station → Shinjuku

activities.title: Shinjuku JR Station — exit East
activities.description: N'EX terminates at Shinjuku JR Station. Use the East Exit for taxi rank. Short taxi (~5 min, ¥1,000) gets you to Yuen — easier than rolling carry-on through the Marunouchi Line transfer to Shinjuku-Gyoemmae, even though Yuen is only 1km away.

activities.title: Onsen Ryokan Yuen Shinjuku check-in
activities.description: ~5 min taxi from Shinjuku JR East Exit (~¥1,000) or 15 min walk if you want to stretch. Drop bags, freshen up, soak the jet lag away in the 18F rooftop open-air onsen before late dinner.

activities.title: Afuri Shinjuku Lumine1
activities.description: Same exact Afuri yuzu-shio ramen experience as the Harajuku original — light, clean, citrus-forward Tokyo first-night classic. Lumine1 B2F at Shinjuku Station (open till 22:00, so 20:30 is the late-arrival sweet spot). 1 stop on the Marunouchi Line from Yuen, or a 20-min walk west through Shinjuku 3-chome.

activities.title: Asakusa Station
activities.description: Metro arrival point for Senso-ji.

activities.title: Senso-ji Temple
activities.description: Tokyo's oldest temple, 7th century. Walk under Kaminarimon lantern. Early arrival = fewer tour buses.

activities.title: Nakamise-dori snacks
activities.description: Ningyo-yaki, agemanju, melon pan from approach-street stalls. Map opens at Asakusa Kibidango Azuma — Street View to scout nearby vendors.

activities.title: Daikokuya Tempura
activities.description: Asakusa tendon institution since 1887.

activities.title: Yanaka Ginza
activities.description: Old Tokyo shopping street, cat-themed shops. Map opens at Yuyake Dandan stairs — Street View to walk the street.
activities.address: Yuyake Dandan stairs, 3 Chome Yanaka, Taito

activities.title: SCAI The Bathhouse
activities.description: 200-year-old bathhouse turned contemporary gallery.

activities.title: Kayaba Coffee
activities.description: 1938 kissaten. Classic egg sandwich.

activities.title: Omoide Yokocho
activities.description: Smoky yakitori alley by Shinjuku JR tracks. Order momo, seseri, sunagimo.

activities.title: Golden Gai
activities.description: 200+ tiny bars. Try Albatross or Cremaster.

activities.title: Shimokitazawa Station
activities.description: Tokyo's vintage capital.

activities.title: Chicago (vintage)
activities.description: Americana, wall of vintage Levi's.

activities.title: Flamingo Shimokita
activities.description: Curated mid-range vintage.

activities.title: Stick Out
activities.description: Everything ¥700.

activities.title: Toyo Hyakkaten
activities.description: 25+ vintage sellers under one roof.

activities.title: Magic Spice (soup curry)
activities.description: Shimokita institution.

activities.title: Koenji Station
activities.description: 15 min on the Chuo Line from Shimokita.

activities.title: Spank! Koenji
activities.description: Y2K, kawaii core.

activities.title: Hayatochiri
activities.description: Vintage kimono fabric.

activities.title: Higashi-Ginza Station
activities.description: Direct exit to Kabukiza Theater.

activities.title: Kabukiza Theatre
activities.description: Single-act ticket (makumi-ken) at window. Audio guide ¥700.

activities.title: Yoyogi Park walk
activities.description: Quick Marunouchi Line ride from Shinjuku-Gyoemmae to Yoyogi-Koen — relaxed morning walk through cedars + autumn leaves before catching the 10:30 Fuji Excursion. Last Tokyo activity before heading to the lake.

activities.title: Meiji Jingu Shrine
activities.description: Major Shinto shrine in a vast forested grove — the counterpart to Senso-ji's Buddhist scale. Quiet at this hour, you'll have the gravel paths almost to yourselves. Allow ~90 min round trip from main torii.

activities.title: Shinjuku Station — bento + coffee
activities.description: ~15 min from Meiji Jingu: walk south to Harajuku Station, JR Yamanote 1 stop to Shinjuku. Grab an ekiben from Ecute Shinjuku or the Lumine bakery — Fuji Excursion boards at ~10:25.

activities.title: Fuji Excursion — board at Shinjuku
activities.description: JR/Fujikyu Fuji Excursion limited express to Kawaguchiko — ~2hr direct, ¥4,200pp, all-reserved (no non-reserved cars). Book exactly 1 month in advance at 10:00 a.m. JST (set Oct 27 calendar reminder). Watch for Mt Fuji views on the right side of the train after Otsuki.

routeToNext.pinned: Fuji Excursion limited express scheduled time

activities.title: Kawaguchiko Station
activities.description: Arrive Kawaguchiko Station 12:30. Phone Ubuya on +81-555-72-1145 — their on-call shuttle runs 14:30-18:00 (~10 min, free). Since you're arriving before 14:30, options: (a) wait ~30 min for shuttle window, (b) taxi (~¥2,000, 10 min) for fastest, (c) Red Line sightseeing bus to 'Kozantei Ubuya' stop (~16 min, ¥420). All three are fine — taxi is cleanest with even a small bag.

activities.title: Kozantei Ubuya check-in
activities.description: Phone Ubuya from Kawaguchiko Station (+81-555-72-1145) for the on-call shuttle — runs 14:30-18:00 free, ~10 min. If you arrive before 14:30, taxi (~¥2,000, 10 min) or Red Line sightseeing bus to the 'Kozantei Ubuya' stop (~16 min, ¥420). Early afternoon = relaxed onsen + lake walk before Kaiseki #1.

activities.title: Kaiseki dinner #1
activities.description: Multi-course at the ryokan.

activities.title: Japanese breakfast
activities.description: Grilled fish, miso soup, tamagoyaki, rice.

activities.title: First onsen
activities.description: Open-air bath with Fuji view.

activities.title: Café Mimi lunch
activities.description: Lakeside sandwiches and coffee.

activities.title: Lake Kawaguchi north shore walk
activities.description: Quiet shoreline, Fuji reflections if calm.

activities.title: Couples massage
activities.description: Booked through the ryokan.

activities.title: Kaiseki dinner #2 · hanafuda night
activities.description: Multi-course dinner at the ryokan. Bring the Nintendo hanafuda deck picked up Day 3 and learn Koi-Koi over sake — watch a quick rules video on the way to dinner. Quiet lake, paper screens, two-player game.

activities.title: Japanese breakfast at the ryokan
activities.description: Grilled fish, miso soup, tamagoyaki, rice. Slow morning at the ryokan after Chureito sunrise.

activities.title: Oshino Hakkai
activities.description: Eight crystal-clear spring-fed ponds.

activities.title: Houtou Fudou
activities.description: Yamanashi miso noodle stew (houtou).

activities.title: Mt Fuji Panoramic Ropeway
activities.description: 3-min cable car to Mt Tenjo viewpoint.

activities.title: Kaiseki dinner at FUFU
activities.description: Final Fuji-night splurge dinner at FUFU. In-suite or in-restaurant kaiseki — seasonal multi-course using Yamanashi ingredients, paired with the private outdoor onsen between courses.

activities.title: Bus from Kawaguchiko
activities.description: Highway bus to Mishima.

activities.title: Mishima Station
activities.description: Transfer to Tokaido Shinkansen.

activities.title: Hotel check-in
activities.description: The Thousand Kyoto.

activities.title: Nishiki Market
activities.description: Kyoto's kitchen — pickles, tofu, skewers. Map opens at Aritsugu knife shop (mid-market anchor) — Street View nearby stalls.

activities.title: Pontocho dinner
activities.description: Lantern alley along the Kamogawa. Map opens at Pontocho-Suishin (mid-alley) — Street View to pick a restaurant.

activities.title: Hanamikoji, Gion
activities.description: Lantern-lit machiya street. Do NOT photograph geiko/maiko. Map opens at Ichiriki Chaya (iconic red ochaya corner).
activities.address: Ichiriki Chaya, 569 Gionmachi Minamigawa, Higashiyama, Kyoto

activities.title: Yoramu sake bar
activities.description: Tiny serious sake bar.

activities.title: % Arabica Higashiyama
activities.description: First stop — minimalist Kyoto coffee with view of Yasaka Pagoda. Leave the hotel ~8 AM, ~20-30 min by taxi/bus.

activities.title: Saryo Tsujiri Gion
activities.description: Matcha breakfast.

activities.title: Kiyomizu-dera
activities.description: Wooden stage over Higashiyama.

activities.title: Hisago oyakodon
activities.description: Chicken + egg over rice.

activities.title: Yumeyakata kimono rental
activities.description: 4 hr rental.

activities.title: Shirakawa geisha district
activities.description: Willow-lined canal. Map opens at Tatsumi Bridge — iconic Shirakawa photo spot.
activities.address: Tatsumi Bridge, Gion Shirakawa, Higashiyama, Kyoto

activities.title: Camellia tea ceremony
activities.description: 45 min English session.

activities.title: Giro Giro Hitoshina kaiseki
activities.description: Reserve ~1 month ahead.

activities.title: Byodo-in Temple
activities.description: UNESCO. ¥10-coin temple.

activities.title: Nakamura Tokichi Honten
activities.description: Matcha soba + parfait.

activities.title: Nara Park
activities.description: 1,200 bowing deer. Shika senbei ¥200.

activities.title: Todai-ji
activities.description: Largest wooden building. 15m Buddha.

activities.title: Kasuga Taisha
activities.description: 3,000 lanterns through forest.

activities.title: Inari Station
activities.description: Sunrise mission — leave The Thousand Kyoto ~06:30. JR Nara line two stops from Kyoto Station.

activities.title: Fushimi Inari Taisha · sunrise hike
activities.description: Timing is everything — at 7 AM the 10K torii path is nearly empty, mystical light, photographers only. Hike to the Yotsutsuji intersection viewpoint (~45 min up, 30 min down). Skip the summit if tired — Yotsutsuji is where the view opens. Free, 24/7.

activities.title: Saga-Arashiyama Station
activities.description: Back to Kyoto Station, switch to JR Sagano line out to Arashiyama (~45 min total from Fushimi).

activities.title: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
activities.description: Still beating the 10 AM tour bus rush. Walk through quickly — atmosphere drops the busier it gets.

activities.title: Otagi Nenbutsuji (1200 stone faces)
activities.description: ~30 min walk uphill from bamboo grove. 1200+ rakan stone figures carved by amateur sculptors 1981–91 — each face is a different emotion: smiling, drinking, meditating, laughing, crying. Quiet, atmospheric, very photogenic.

activities.title: Adashino Nenbutsuji (8,000 Buddhas)
activities.description: 5 min walk back down from Otagi. Thousands of small weathered stone Buddhas memorializing the forgotten dead, plus a small bamboo grove behind the main hall. Less famous than the main Arashiyama grove — but you'll have it almost to yourselves.

activities.title: Lunch in Arashiyama town
activities.description: Walk back down toward Togetsukyo. Yudofu (tofu hot pot) is the Arashiyama signature — try Shoraian or Yudofu Sagano. Or grab matcha soba at Arashiyama Yoshimura with bridge views.

activities.title: Togetsukyo Bridge
activities.description: Crossing the Katsura river — classic Arashiyama postcard. Short walk after lunch.

activities.title: Chushojima Station
activities.description: JR Sagano line back to Kyoto, transfer Keihan to Chushojima. ~45 min.

activities.title: Gekkeikan Okura Sake Museum
activities.description: Edo-era brewery from 1637, the Japan-sake heartland. 5-min walk from Chushojima Station. Open 9:30-16:30 (last entry 16:00, so leave Arashiyama early enough). Tasting flight at the end: 3 small cups of Gekkeikan sake + a small bottle to take home — included in ¥600 admission. The brick-and-wood riverside brewery building + canal walk between the station and museum is the real photo moment. Souvenir bottle and on-site shop if you want to buy something to take home.

activities.title: Bee's Knees · Kyoto speakeasy
activities.description: Last Kyoto nightcap. Quiet, tucked-away speakeasy, classic cocktails. Get dinner around Kawaramachi area first, then walk over. Cash preferred.

activities.title: Hotel check-in
activities.description: Cross Hotel Osaka.

activities.title: Shinsaibashi arcade
activities.description: Covered shopping street running 600m. Map opens mid-arcade — Street View to walk it.

activities.title: Mizuno okonomiyaki
activities.description: Michelin Bib Gourmand.

activities.title: Kushikatsu Daruma Dotonbori
activities.description: ONE-DIP rule!

activities.title: Hozenji Yokocho
activities.description: Moss-covered Buddha, tiny bars. Map opens at Hozen-ji Temple (moss Buddha).

activities.title: Kuromon Ichiba Market
activities.description: Uni, wagyu, oysters.

activities.title: Osaka Castle Park
activities.description: Castle keep + museum + park grounds. Fall foliage peaks late November — momiji + ginkgo around the moats. Grab a quick lunch inside the park (food carts) or at Jo-Terrace Osaka before climbing to the observation deck.

activities.title: Tsutenkaku Tower
activities.description: 1956 retro neon.

activities.title: Janjan Yokocho
activities.description: Lantern alley. Map opens at the entrance arch.
activities.address: Janjan Yokocho arcade entrance, Naniwa, Osaka

activities.title: Kushikatsu Daruma Shinsekai
activities.description: Original location.

activities.title: Bar Nayuta · speakeasy
activities.description: Dotonbori-area speakeasy. Hidden door, classic + creative cocktails, intimate bar. Walk 15 min back from Shinsekai to Namba. Cocktails ¥1500–2500. Cash preferred.

activities.title: Hotel check-in (finale)
activities.description: Shibuya Tokyu REI Hotel.

activities.title: About Life Coffee Brewers
activities.description: Tiny specialty stand by the Onibus Coffee team — Reddit favorite for a fast, perfect Shibuya pour-over or flat white. Open till 6pm, so this is the late-afternoon coffee anchor before the Takarazuka show.

activities.title: Takarazuka Tokyo Theater
activities.description: All-female revue. ¥4–12K.

activities.title: Mori Art Museum
activities.description: 53rd floor Roppongi Hills.

activities.title: Lunch in Roppongi Hills
activities.description: Roppongi Hills mall and the surrounding streets have everything — Toraya for sweets, Tsurutontan for udon, Bricolage Bread & Co for casual.

activities.title: National Art Center
activities.description: Kurokawa's wavy glass facade — the building itself is the attraction even if the rotating exhibit doesn't grab you. Free to wander the atrium + cone-shaped cafe inside.

activities.title: Shibuya Scramble Crossing
activities.description: Walk across Tokyo's iconic intersection — 3,000 people per light cycle. Take the photo from the Starbucks above (Tsutaya 2F, free). 3 min walk to the Hachiko statue. 20-30 min total before going up Sky.

activities.title: Shibuya Sky
activities.description: Sunset rooftop — you'll be looking DOWN at the Scramble you just walked. Pre-book the 5pm slot online.

activities.title: Sushi Hashimoto omakase
activities.description: Book 4–6 weeks ahead.

activities.title: Bar High Five · nightcap
activities.description: Hidetsugu Ueno's legendary Ginza bar — 5 min walk from Hashimoto. Classic cocktails, no music, intimate counter. Reservation strongly recommended. ¥3-4K per cocktail. The full-stop on the best night of the trip.
activities.address: Efflore Ginza 5 Building B1, 5-4-15 Ginza, Chuo City

activities.title: Tsukiji Outer Market
activities.description: The original fish-market site (the auction moved to Toyosu but the outer market stayed). Best between 7-9 AM. Sushi-for-breakfast vibes, knife shops, tamago skewers, tuna nigiri at counter pace. Walking distance to Ginza for the rest of the day.
activities.address: Tsukiji Outer Market, 4 Chome Tsukiji, Chuo City

activities.title: Endo Sushi (Tsukiji)
activities.description: Sushi for breakfast — the Tsukiji ritual. ¥3000-5000 pp set menus, sit at the counter, watch the chef work. Lines move fast 8-9 AM.

activities.title: Dover Street Market Ginza
activities.description: 7 floors of high concept.

activities.title: G. Itoya stationery
activities.description: 8 floors of paper goods.

activities.title: Pokemon Center DX Tokyo
activities.description: The flagship Pokemon Center — Nihombashi Takashimaya 5F, 5 min walk from Tokyo Station Yaesu side. Pokemon TCG, plushies, exclusive Tokyo-only merch. Cafe attached (reservation-only, may be booked). Browsing 60-90 min is enough.
activities.address: Nihombashi Takashimaya S.C. East Bldg 5F, 2-11-2 Nihombashi, Chuo-ku, Tokyo

activities.title: Cat Street vintage cluster (Harajuku)
activities.description: Better fit-odds than Shimokita for tall/broader bodies — these are US-sourced. Walk Cat Street + side alleys: 2nd Street (consistent sizing), BerBerJin (Western Levi's including bigger waists), Komehyo Harajuku (used designer, accurate sizing), Atlantis Vintage (US/EU vintage). Skip Kinji/Pin Nap (small Japanese-only sizing).

activities.title: Shelter / Basement Bar live house
activities.description: Underground music. ¥3–5K cover.

checklist.title: Yamato luggage forward · NRT → Yuen Shinjuku (Nov 24)
checklist.detail: Drop checked bags at the Yamato Transport (Kuroneko Takkyubin) counter in NRT Arrivals after customs. Next-day delivery to Yuen Shinjuku (Nov 25 evening). ~¥2,500-3,500 per large bag. Carry only an overnight bag with PJs, toiletries, change of clothes for Night 1.

checklist.title: Kozantei Ubuya · 3 nights (Nov 27–29)
checklist.detail: Direct booking or Relux. Mention honeymoon.

checklist.title: Tokyo hotels · 3 nights start + 3 nights end
checklist.detail: Same hotel both stays = easier baggage drop.

checklist.title: Omakase splurge · Day 14 (Dec 7)
checklist.detail: Pocket Concierge / TableCheck. Options: Hashimoto, Tokami, Manten.

checklist.title: Camellia tea ceremony · Day 8 (6 PM English)
Yamato luggage forward · Yuen Shinjuku → Hotel TOU Kyoto (Nov 29)
checklist.detail: Drop bags at Yuen Shinjuku front desk Nov 26 evening or Nov 27 AM (~3 PM Yamato cutoff). ~¥2-3K per bag. Skip carrying through Fuji entirely — bags meet you in Kyoto on Nov 30. Travel with small overnight bag for Fuji Excursion + ryokan nights.

checklist.title: Yamato luggage forward · Ubuya → The Thousand Kyoto (Nov 30)
checklist.detail: Drop at Ubuya front desk Nov 29 evening. Saves you wrangling bags through the bus + 2 shinkansen transfers Mishima → Kyoto. ~¥2-3K per bag.

checklist.title: Yamato luggage forward · Cross Osaka → Shibuya Tokyu REI Tokyo (Dec 6)
checklist.detail: Drop at Cross Hotel front desk Dec 5 evening. Skip the 2.5-hr Nozomi luggage hassle + the post-2020 oversized-bag reservation requirement. ~¥2-3K per bag.

wishlist.title: My Only Fragrance · custom perfume
wishlist.description: 90-min experience: smell ~20 scents, pick 4-5, they blend a custom perfume for you both, named. ~¥9,500 pp. Could fit Day 8 afternoon (squeeze before/after kimono) or Day 11 morning. Walk-in possible but reservations smarter.

wishlist.title: Ring engraving (Glanta or Ginto, Kyoto)
wishlist.description: Walk-in ring engraving — ¥3-8K depending on length. Bring rings you already own + a word you collected on the trip. Plan: pick the word during Days 4-10, get engraved before leaving Kyoto Day 11.

wishlist.title: Bees Knees · Kyoto speakeasy
wishlist.description: Tucked-away Kyoto speakeasy, classic cocktails, intimate. Alternative or backup to Yoramu sake (Day 7) if you want a cocktail night instead.

wishlist.title: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
wishlist.description: Iconic photo if you want a temple-heavy day. Skipped in your current plan to avoid temple fatigue, but you could squeeze in Day 11 morning before Osaka travel.

wishlist.title: Chureito Pagoda sunrise
wishlist.description: Iconic Mt Fuji + pagoda + autumn-leaves shot. Sunrise on Nov 28-29 is ~06:42, so you'd wake ~05:30 and taxi 10 min from Ubuya (3km, ~¥1500). Climb ~400 steps to the observation deck. Optional — decide morning-of based on weather + how you're feeling. Either Day 5 or Day 6 early morning works. Coords: 35.50119, 138.80139.

wishlist.title: Japanese knife shop (Kappabashi or Tsukiji)
wishlist.description: Kama-Asa Shoten (the planned one, Kappabashi) is a 10-min walk from Senso-ji — could fit Day 2 morning between Senso-ji and Yanaka. Or Tsukiji Outer Market on Day 15 has Aritsugu, Sugimoto, Tsubaya, Kiya — high-quality knife shops right next to where you're already eating sushi. Either is a casual catch-it-when-out option, no need to plan around.
